title: Consortium (post-nationalization oil operating group) Disbursements in Iran, 1954-1962 -- incl. aggregate wages/salaries paid
source_org: US Bureau of Mines Information Circular 8203 (1963), Table 1 (p.16) + narrative summary table (p.15)
extraction_method: pdftoppm -r 150 render to PNG (PDF pages 24-25 of data/raw/pahlavi-era-primary-extraction/usbm1963-source-pdf/) + Read tool visual verification. No OCR/pdftotext used (source has no text layer). Every cell cross-checked by manual subtotal arithmetic where the source provides subtotals (all checks passed except one flagged elsewhere in this round's NIOC personnel table).
time_coverage: 1954-1962 (1954 is a partial year, Oct 29-Dec 31 only)
units: GBP (pounds sterling); percentages for the GNP/forex/govt-expenditure ratio rows
notes: DIRECT HIT on this round's user-brief item 'historical wage data for specific well-documented occupations -- oil-industry workers' wages are unusually well documented due to the Consortium Agreement era': the 'Wages and salaries' row is a genuine, authoritative, primary-source AGGREGATE annual wage/salary bill paid by the oil Consortium in Iran, 1955-1962 (GBP 15.6M in 1955 rising to GBP 28.3M in 1962). This is an aggregate paid-out total, NOT a per-worker wage rate -- a future harmonization pass COULD divide this by the employment counts in the sibling usbm1963-oil-industry-personnel-by-category-1955-1961 dataset to derive an implied average annual wage per Consortium employee, but that division was deliberately NOT performed in this raw-data-landing round (would mix two different tables' figures into a derived statistic, better done explicitly and flagged during a data/processed/ harmonization pass, not silently in data/raw/). Overlap-risk note: this table's 'Payments to Iranian Government' and 'Income tax' rows are fiscal/government-revenue-adjacent, which could overlap with concurrent agents working on 'government financial archives (Majlis/CBI)' -- included here anyway because the anchor and organizing logic of the whole table is the OIL CONSORTIUM's disbursements (a company-side accounting view), not a government-budget-side document, and the wages/salaries and social-insurance rows (the actual point of this dataset) are unambiguously outside any government-finance agent's likely scope.
